novel_features.name: Ride + FIT archive
novel_features.description: Download every workout record plus its raw FIT file into a resumable local directory tree so you own a permanent backup.
novel_features.rationale: Only possible because the local mirror knows every workout and its file URL; FIT downloads are rate-limit-exempt, so a full back-catalog pull is cheap.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user wants their rides backed up or exported off Wahoo's cloud — the app has no bulk export.

novel_features.name: Training load (Fitness/Fatigue/Form)
novel_features.description: Compute CTL (Fitness), ATL (Fatigue), and TSB (Form) time series from your synced rides — the analysis TrainingPeaks charges for.
novel_features.rationale: The Wahoo API stores raw per-ride metrics but computes nothing; this runs the exponentially-weighted load math locally over the SQLite mirror.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when the user asks how fit, fatigued, or fresh they are, or whether to push or rest this week.
novel_features.group: Training analytics the app won't compute

novel_features.name: Recent-window digest
novel_features.description: One-shot rollup of the last N days: ride count, distance, time, ascent, work, and load delta — pipeable to any tool.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates the local workout mirror in-process; no single API call returns a windowed total and the app shows one ride at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for 'how much did I ride this week/month/year' (--days 365 covers year-in-review).
novel_features.group: Training analytics the app won't compute

novel_features.name: Personal bests
novel_features.description: All-time and per-period records for distance, ascent, average power, duration, and work from your ride summaries.
novel_features.rationale: Mechanical max/sort over the NULL-safe-parsed summary fields in the local mirror; the API offers no records endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this for PRs and bragging-rights records; note it uses ride summaries, not per-second streams.
novel_features.group: Training analytics the app won't compute

novel_features.name: FTP progression
novel_features.description: Dated timeline of your FTP and critical power from power-zone records and sync snapshots, with watts/kg when your weight is known.
novel_features.rationale: Joins power_zones records and per-sync FTP snapshots in local SQLite to reconstruct progression the API never exposes as a series.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the user wants to see whether their FTP is trending up over a training block.
novel_features.group: Training analytics the app won't compute

novel_features.name: Offline route finder
novel_features.description: Filter your saved routes by distance band, climbing, and proximity to a point — query a route library the app only scrolls.
novel_features.rationale: Range filters plus a haversine distance run over the routes mirror's stored lat/lng and elevation fields; the API has no route filtering.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when the user wants 'a ~100km route under 1000m of climbing near here' from their own saved routes.

narrative.headline: Every Wahoo Cloud API capability, plus the local training database and offline analysis Wahoo's app never gives you.
narrative.value_prop: Sync your Wahoo rides, routes, plans, and power zones into a local SQLite mirror, then do what the app can't: back up every ride and FIT file, compute Fitness/Fatigue/Form and FTP progression, find a saved route by distance and elevation, and roll up any window of training. Agent-native output (--json/--agent/--select) makes it scriptable end to end.
narrative.auth_narrative: Auth is OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code with PKCE. A Sandbox app works immediately against your own account with NO Wahoo approval — approval is only needed for higher rate limits or accessing other people's data. Register an app at https://developers.wahooligan.com/applications/new with the redirect URI http://localhost:8085/callback, pick your scopes, then set WAHOO_CLIENT_ID and WAHOO_CLIENT_SECRET (or pass --client-id/--client-secret) and run 'wahoo auth login' to authorize in your browser (use --port to change the callback port; sign in to Wahoo however you normally do, including Sign in with Google). Access tokens last 2 hours and refresh automatically (refresh tokens are single-use); you can also paste a pre-obtained token via WAHOO_CLOUD_OAUTH2. Revoke this app's access any time with 'wahoo permissions'. Run 'wahoo-pp-cli auth setup' for the full step-by-step.

quickstart.comment: Confirm config and rate-limit status; no auth needed.
Authorize in the browser (needs an approved Wahoo app).
Pull your data into the local SQLite mirror.
Download every ride record and FIT file locally.

troubleshoots.fix: Run 'wahoo auth login' to reauthorize; access tokens expire after 2 hours and refresh tokens are single-use.
Sandbox apps allow only 250 requests/day; pace with 'wahoo sync --max-pages N' or wait for the window to reset. FIT downloads (backup) are exempt.
Wahoo must approve your app first — register at developers.wahooligan.com/applications/new and wait for the pending status to clear.

troubleshoots.symptom: load or bests shows no data
troubleshoots.fix: Run 'wahoo sync --resources workouts' first; analysis commands read the local mirror.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an agent needs a user's Wahoo ride history, FIT files, routes, plans, or power zones, or wants training analytics — load/form, FTP progression, personal bests, recent totals — computed locally. It keeps a local SQLite mirror, so repeated queries are fast and offline. It is the right tool for backing up rides, scripting training dashboards, and pushing routes or plans to an ELEMNT.
anti_triggers: Do not use for live Bluetooth control of the ELEMNT device — this is the Cloud API and only sees data that has synced to Wahoo's cloud.
Do not use for per-second power/HR stream analysis or mean-maximal power curves — the API exposes only per-workout summary averages, not streams.
Do not use to push activities to Strava or TrainingPeaks — it talks only to Wahoo's cloud.

recipes.title: Inspect a ride's key metrics for an agent
recipes.explanation: Narrows a deeply nested workout (with embedded summary) to just the fields an agent needs, avoiding tens of KB of payload.

recipes.title: Back up an entire ride history
recipes.explanation: Mirrors all workouts, then downloads each one's FIT file into a resumable local archive.

recipes.title: Check form before a hard week
recipes.explanation: Returns the Fitness/Fatigue/Form series so an agent can advise whether to add intensity or rest.

recipes.title: Pick a Saturday route
recipes.explanation: Filters saved routes to ~90-110km within 25km of a point using stored geo and elevation fields.
